channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Dozens of religious groups sue to stop Trump admin from arresting migrants in places of worship
item.description: A coalition of 27 Christian and Jewish groups filed a lawsuit challenging a Trump administration action allowing immigration agents to make arrests in places of worship.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia releases imprisoned American Marc Fogel in what US calls a step toward the end of Ukraine war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Marc Fogel, an American history teacher who was deemed wrongfully detained by Russia, has been released and returned to the U.S. in what the White House described as a diplomatic thaw that could advance negotiations to end the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sotomayor says presidents have largely followed rule of law, offers words of caution
item.description: U.S. Supreme Court Justice Sonia Sotomayor said presidents have historically obeyed judicial rulings with a few exceptions while advocating for the court to move cautiously to maintain a system of checks and balances.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 4 FEMA employees are fired over payments to reimburse New York City for hotel costs for migrants
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Four federal employees were fired Tuesday over payments to reimburse New York City for hotel costs for migrants, Department of Homeland Security officials said.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine selling arms to cartels? Tucker Carlson claims rebutted
item.description: Carlson claims U.S. intelligence agencies know about the billions in transactions to arm “our actual enemies.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Appeals court rejects Trump administration push to reinstate spending freezes on grants and loans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal appeals court on Tuesday rejected a Trump administration push to reinstate a sweeping pause on federal funding, a decision that comes after a judge found the administration had not fully obeyed an earlier order. The Boston-based 1st U.S.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gov. Hochul reacts to pending dismissal of corruption case against Mayor Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- The pending dismissal of the corruption case against New York City Mayor Eric Adams has partners in running the city and political opponents raising concerns about whether Adams can run the city. Gov. Kathy Hochul even said she does not know if the mayor is “compromised.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: FEMA rebels fired
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-FEMA worker who directed staff to avoid homes with Trump signs violated Hatch Act, agency says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal agency is seeking disciplinary action against a former Federal Emergency Management Agency worker who directed workers helping hurricane survivors to avoid homes with yard signs supporting President Donald Trump, according to a complaint filed Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he'll abide by court orders that block parts of his agenda
item.description: President Trump said Tuesday he would abide by court rulings if they blocked parts of his agenda amid uproar over comments from some allies about defying the legal system.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: With federal money at risk, Minnesota’s public colleges stress high stakes of state budget debate
item.description: The University of Minnesota and Minnesota State Colleges and Universities systems are hoping for extra allocations from the Legislature this year. They know it’s a challenge given the state’s leaner budget.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Scientists expect major 'medical breakthroughs' despite Trump's cap on NIH research funding
item.description: The Trump administration's move to slash overhead costs associated with federally funded research has generated immense backlash, but doctors who spoke to Fox News Digital praised the move, arguing it will help "optimize" how taxpayer dollars are used when it comes to scientific resea
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk defends DOGE alongside Trump in striking Oval Office moment
item.description: Elon Musk on Tuesday defended the work of the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) alongside President Trump, who invited Musk to speak and sat at the resolute desk as the tech mogul gave a lengthy address.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP lawmakers' bill tackles child trafficking crisis at border
item.description: Republican lawmakers from the House and Senate are joining forces to push legislation aimed at properly vetting sponsors of unaccompanied migrant children.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge adjusts ruling blocking Musk, DOGE from Treasury Department payment systems
item.description: A federal judge Tuesday clarified that a ruling preventing Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) from accessing the Treasury Department’s payment systems does not extend to Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada lawmaker moves to put Indigenous Peoples' Day on Columbus Day
item.description: Nevada law says Indigenous Peoples Day falls on Aug. 9, but a lawmaker is trying to change that with AB144, which would set the observation on the second Monday in October — the same day as Colombus Day.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 27 religious groups sue Trump administration over immigration enforcement policy
item.description: 27 religious groups are suing the federal government in response to the Trump administration’s policy giving immigration agents more leeway to make arrests at “sensitive locations” like churches.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Ventre, MPR News

item.title: The pope rebukes Trump over migrant deportations and refutes VP Vance’s theology
item.description: In a strongly worded letter to Catholic bishops in the United States, Pope Francis is taking to task the Trump administration’s stance on migrants, calling it a “major crisis.
item.dc:creator: Jason DeRose, MPR News

item.title: Pentagon changes Fort Liberty's name back to Fort Bragg
item.description: The Pentagon will change the name of the North Carolina military base Fort Liberty back to Fort Bragg, fulfilling a campaign pledge by President Trump while honoring a different eponym.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Durbin calls for investigation into Patel's role in FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) is asking the Department of Justice’s (DOJ) inspector general to investigate “highly credible information” that Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to head the FBI, is already directing a “purge” of the bureau before he’s been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Pinellas lawmaker files bill to adopt 'Gulf of America' name in Florida statutes
item.description: A Pinellas County lawmaker filed a bill that would change every mention of the Gulf of Mexico in Florida state statutes to the "Gulf of America" name proposed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hegseth says DOGE welcome at Pentagon as Defense Department reviews military posture globally
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Pentagon did not push same "globalist agendas" as USAID but welcomed DOGE "partnership" to find billions in waste.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jordan pledges to take in sick Palestinian children as Trump backpedals on aid threat
item.description: King Abdullah II of Jordan pledged to take in 2,000 sick Palestinian children from the Gaza Strip on Tuesday amid pressure from President Trump for his country to take in Palestinians from the enclave.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: King of Jordan urges patience as Trump presses Gaza takeover
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday dug in on his vision for a U.S. takeover of the Gaza Strip as he sat alongside King Abdullah II of Jordan, who expressed reservations about the idea.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What’s next for Jake Sullivan, a Minnesotan and Biden’s former National Security Advisor
item.description: As National Security Advisor, Jake Sullivan helped shape Biden’s foreign policy agenda and was involved in the U.S. response to events like the Russian invasion of Ukraine and the Israel-Hamas war.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Alanna Elder, MPR News

item.title: Eric Adams reiterates innocence amid DOJ order
item.description: The Justice Department ordered federal prosecutors to drop corruption charges against the New York City mayor.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s two cents: President orders U.S. Treasury to ditch the penny to save money
item.description: President Donald Trump says he has directed the Treasury Department to stop minting new pennies, citing the rising cost of producing the one-cent coin.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump says he’s firing Kennedy Center board of trustees members and naming himself chair
item.description: President Donald Trump says he’s firing members of the board of trustees for the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts and naming himself chair.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs order to establish White House Faith Office
item.description: Trump directed the office to identify ways to reduce burdens on the free exercise of religion and direct all federal agencies to designate a “faith liaison” within 90 days.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada consumers could get a break on egg prices
item.description: Just about everyone buys eggs, and higher prices haven't gone unnoticed in Carson City, where a law passed in 2021 is partly responsible for the eggflation.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
